description: §55.1 (D-FORM-1) — an unknown bare-attribute validator predicate (`lenght`, a typo for `length`) is REJECTED at compile with E-TYPE-031. The universal-core vocabulary is closed at fourteen (§55.1 line 32517); the only escape hatch is `custom(fn)`. Regression guard: this was formerly a SILENT pass-through — codegen emitted `_scrml_validator_fire("lenght",…)`, the runtime pushed `undefined` into the field's errors list, and the field went PERMANENTLY invalid with no diagnostic at all.
rationale: §55.1 closes the validator vocabulary at fourteen universal-core predicates plus `custom(fn)`. Any other predicate name is a typo or a non-first-class predicate and must be rejected — silently passing it through produces a field that can never validate. No dedicated §34 code exists for an unknown predicate name; the E-TYPE-031 family (already used for every validator arity / arg-shape rejection in this checker) carries it.
